label: break.shatter-become (Y breaks — Y comes apart into many small pieces, with no breaker said; as in "the figurine broke", NOT as in "he broke the glass")
pattern: inchoative counterpart of break.shatter; distinct FrameNet frame (Breaking_apart vs Cause_to_fragment) => distinct concept per R3's hard frame boundary (design §1.2)
gloss: something happened to Y at some time; after this time Y is not one something; there are many small somethings.
notes: Authored fresh (no v0 counterpart; kernel-v0 had only the causative word-level break). Cross-reference (annotation only): causative sibling urn:kernel-v1:break.shatter. FN already separates Cause_to_fragment from Breaking_apart, and R3(c) independently forbids clustering a causative with its inchoative. v-00337070 (surface-crack) shares the frame family but describes a surface-only fracture the many-small-pieces clause would misdescribe; it stays excluded (R3(b) truthful-coverage).

sense.glossQuote: become separated into pieces or fragments; "The figurine broke"
